stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Llama indicates relevance to the current state of large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of 'meta-llama' indicates relevance to major industry players in the LLM space.

stories.title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The New York Times is reportedly challenging Microsoft's copyright claims related to a supercomputer built for OpenAI.
The shift in legal focus follows a recent Supreme Court ruling that was unfavorable to Sony in copyright matters.
This indicates ongoing and high-stakes legal battles concerning AI training data and intellectual property rights.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function in language tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI capabilities on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating active development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging applications with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: South Korea is planning a large-scale military transition to drone warfare.
The entire half-million-strong military will be trained to utilize drones as a 'universal combat tool'.
This signals a significant shift in military doctrine towards autonomous and unmanned systems.

stories.title: When Does Combining Language Models Help? A Co-Failure Ceiling on Routing, Voting, and Mixture-of-Agents Across 67 Frontier Models
stories.author: Josef Chen
bullet_points: The paper introduces a theoretical upper bound (a 'Co-Failure Ceiling') on the performance gains of multi-model LLM systems (like routing or voting).
The ceiling dictates that accuracy cannot exceed 1 minus beta, where beta is the rate at which all constituent models fail on the same query.
The study highlights a limitation in current diagnostic methods (like pairwise error correlation) for accurately identifying this critical failure rate (beta).

stories.title: Prompt Injection in Automated Résumé Screening with Large Language Models: Single and Multi-Injection Settings
stories.author: Stefanus Jasin
bullet_points: The research investigates prompt injection attacks specifically within LLM-powered automated résumé screening systems.
Prompt injection is defined as subtle, self-promotional text designed to manipulate LLM evaluations without adding new qualifications.
Experiments demonstrate that this injection technique reliably improves applicant rankings, especially when résumé quality is similar across candidates.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning without Ground-Truth Solutions can Improve LLMs
stories.author: Yuxiong He
bullet_points: Introduces RiVER, a new framework for training Large Language Models (LLMs) using Reinforcement Learning (RL) without requiring ground-truth solutions.
Addresses a key limitation in current RLVR methods where reliance on ground-truth restricts applicability to unknown-solution tasks.
Utilizes deterministic execution feedback as continuous-valued supervision for score-based optimization tasks.

stories.title: Hallucination in World Models is Predictable and Preventable
stories.author: Xiaolong Wang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical issue of 'hallucination' in generative world models, where simulations become visually fluent but diverge from real-world dynamics.
The authors hypothesize that hallucinations occur primarily in sparsely covered regions of the state-action space, suggesting a data-centric solution.
They introduce MMBench2, a large-scale dataset designed to test and guide the detection and mitigation of these model hallucinations.

stories.title: Previewing GPT‑5.6 Sol: a next-generation model
stories.author: minimaxir
bullet_points: The news announces a preview of GPT-5.6 Sol, indicating the development of a next-generation large language model.
The announcement points to a direct link on OpenAI's deployment safety platform for access.
This signals continued, rapid advancement in the state-of-the-art in generative AI.

stories.title: U.S. allows Anthropic to release Mythos AI to ‘trusted’ US organizations
stories.author: bobrenjc93
bullet_points: The U.S. government has granted Anthropic permission to release its Mythos AI model.
The release is restricted to 'trusted' U.S. organizations, indicating controlled deployment.
This signals a growing trend of government oversight and controlled access for advanced AI models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes common ML frameworks and formats like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Antibiotic "megacluster" discovery provides new strategy to fight superbugs
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: Discovery of an antibiotic 'megacluster' offers a new approach against superbugs.
The finding is viewed as a significant step in replenishing the global antibiotic supply.
This represents an advance in the ongoing battle against antibiotic-resistant bacteria.

stories.title: Language-Based Digital Twins for Elderly Cognitive Assistance
stories.author: Hiroko H. Dodge
bullet_points: Proposes a language-based digital twin framework for personalized healthcare.
Utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs) to mimic the conversational behavior of elderly individuals.
Aims to leverage language patterns as non-invasive biomarkers for early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI).

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and limited generalization of small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent tasks.
A new method, Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU), is introduced to enhance autonomous task planning for web agents.
The work focuses on enabling GUI agents to assist humans with repetitive tasks by improving how they decompose complex actions.

stories.title: AI Healthcare Chatbots as Information Infrastructure: A Large-Scale Study of User-Reported Breakdowns
stories.author: Masooda Bashir
bullet_points: The study analyzes over 15,000 user reviews across 59 AI healthcare chatbots to assess real-world performance.
Three major recurring breakdowns were identified: access barriers/service unreliability, and user experience/interaction quality.
The research highlights the need for more rigorous study on the impact of AI chatbots in health information seeking.

stories.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of unifying diverse capabilities (T2I, local/global editing) in modern image generation models.
Identifies a core problem where editing tasks often degrade base T2I performance or conflict with each other.
Introduces 'DanceOPD' as a new method designed to effectively compose and align these conflicting image generation capabilities.

stories.title: When are likely answers right? On Sequence Probability and Correctness in LLMs
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: The paper investigates the fundamental relationship between sequence probability and factual correctness in Large Language Models (LLMs).
It analyzes how various LLM decoding methods shift probability mass toward potential outputs.
The research aims to quantify when the model's predicted sequence probability actually aligns with a correct answer.

stories.title: Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers
stories.author: Jeong Joon Park
bullet_points: Neural surrogate models for solving PDEs are fast but lack mechanisms to correct constraint violations or extrapolate reliably.
Existing hybrid methods improve physical correctness by targeting the PDE residual but suffer from the computational cost of classical optimizers.
The research proposes a new approach (implied by 'We show, theore') to address the limitations of current neural PDE solvers.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and limited generalization of small, cost-efficient Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) used for GUI agent tasks.
A novel method called Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU) is introduced to enhance autonomous task planning.
The goal is to improve the ability of small, privacy-preserving agents to handle complex, repetitive GUI tasks across different websites.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent task planning.
It introduces the PEEU method to enhance autonomous experience exploration and utilize hindsight for better task decomposition.
The goal is to improve the planning and cross-website generalization capabilities of open-source agents for repetitive GUI tasks.

stories.title: Beyond Surface Forms: A Comprehensive, Mechanism-Oriented Taxonomy of Indirect Linguistic Encoding for LLM-Based Coded Language Detection
stories.author: Paul Jen-Hwa Hu
bullet_points: Proposes a mechanism-oriented taxonomy for Indirect Linguistic Encoding (ILE) used to evade moderation.
Addresses the rise of 'algospeak' and adversarial obfuscation on social media platforms.
Aims to categorize the underlying linguistic operations rather than the communicative goals of coded language.

stories.title: How Surprising Is Historical Italian to Language Models? Tokenization Tax, Comprehension Tax, and a Simple Mitigation
stories.author: Maria Levchenko
bullet_points: Investigates the difficulty LLMs have processing historical Italian, moving beyond a monolithic view of language barriers.
Proposes a diagnostic framework to decompose historical language difficulty into four dimensions.
Identifies specific challenges such as tokenization cost and predictive uncertainty (surprisal) in historical text processing.

stories.title: LMs as Task-Specific Knowledge Bases: An Interpretability Analysis
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The paper investigates the hypothesis that Large Language Models (LMs) function as task-specific knowledge bases.
It examines whether LMs maintain the crucial knowledge base property of providing consistent results for the same fact across different queries.
The findings indicate that LMs encode factual knowledge in a manner that is dependent on the specific task they are being used for.

stories.title: AI Didn't Invent Slop. It Only Made It Infinite.
stories.author: copyleftdev
bullet_points: The article argues that AI is not the originator of 'disposable culture' or low-quality content.
AI's primary impact is removing the final bottleneck that previously limited the volume of such content.
This shift necessitates a re-evaluation of the role and responsibilities of engineers.

stories.title: How We Govern Three AI Agents With Five Plain-Text Files
stories.author: dyclgtm
bullet_points: The article discusses a practical approach to managing and governing multiple AI agents.
It highlights the use of a minimalist configuration method, specifically relying on only five plain-text files.
This suggests a focus on simplicity and maintainability in complex multi-agent system design.

stories.title: I gave Claude SSH access to my server — here's the consent gate that makes it safe
stories.author: mhasnainjafri
bullet_points: The article discusses the practical utility and inherent risks of granting AI assistants (specifically Claude) direct command-line access to a server.
It focuses on the implementation of a 'consent gate' mechanism designed to mitigate security risks associated with AI server access.
This topic sits at the intersection of practical AI application and critical cybersecurity concerns in DevOps/MLOps.
